content: Preventive care value assessment considers: (1) Number needed to screen/treat - mammography NNS=500-1,000 to prevent one breast cancer death over 10 years; (2) Overdiagnosis risk - PSA screening detects many indolent prostate cancers leading to unnecessary treatment harms; (3) Age appropriateness - colonoscopy screening generally stops at 75-85 depending on comorbidities; (4) Risk stratification - lung cancer screening only for 50-80 year olds with ≥20 pack-year smoking history. Low-value preventive services include routine vitamin D screening in asymptomatic adults, annual ECGs in low-risk patients, and ovarian cancer screening in average-risk women. Choosing Wisely campaign identifies 80+ preventive services lacking evidence.
